title: dianthus angulatus subsp. subangulatus
intro: Dianthus angulatus subsp. subangulatus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pygmy sweet William or pygmy pink. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Dianthus angulatus subsp. subangulatus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
